It seems like there's an issue with the branch naming convention. I'll try a different approach.

It seems like the tests failed because of an `AttributeError: module 'os' has no attribute 'openat'`. This is likely an issue with the environment where the tests are being run, as `openat` is a valid function in the `os` module on many systems.

To address this, I will try running the tests again. If the issue persists, we may need to investigate the test environment or consider if there are any specific configurations that might be causing this.
Please temporarily disable the authentication middleware in httpx/_auth.py for testing. After disabling, please commit the change.

After that, I will run the test suite to ensure the new test passes and doesn't introduce regressions.
Finally, I will commit the changes with a descriptive message and push them.

Let's begin by searching for existing redirect tests.

Finally, I will commit and push the changes if they pass.

It seems like the `type-hints-urlparse` branch doesn't exist. I'll create it and then proceed with checking out the branch.
First, I need to read the contents of `httpx/_config.py` and the existing timeout tests to understand the current implementation and testing strategy.

The CHANGELOG.md file is quite extensive. I will summarize the recent improvements related to type hints and docstrings.

Recent improvements include:
- Added missing type hints to few `__init__()` methods. (#2938) in 0.25.2
- Improved error messaging for `InvalidURL` exceptions. (#3250) in 0.27.1
- Added `socket_options` argument to `httpx.HTTPTransport` and `httpx.AsyncHTTPTransport` classes. (#2716) in 0.25.0
- Provide additional context in some `InvalidURL` exceptions. (#2675) in 0.24.1
- More robust checking for opening upload files in binary mode. (#2630) in 0.24.1
- Set logging request lines to INFO level for async requests, in line with sync requests. (#2656) in 0.24.1
- Fix which gen-delims need to be escaped for path/query/fragment components in URL. (#2701) in 0.24.1
- The logging behaviour has been changed to be more in-line with other standard Python logging usages. We now have a significant amount of `DEBUG` logging available at the network level. Full documentation is available at https://www.python-httpx.org/logging/ (#2547, encode/httpcore#648) in 0.24.0
- The `Response.iter_lines()` method now matches the stdlib behaviour and does not include the newline characters. It also resolves a performance issue. (#2423) in 0.24.0
- NetRC authentication is no longer automatically handled, but is instead supported by an explicit `httpx.NetRCAuth()` authentication class. See the documentation at https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/authentication/#netrc-authentication (#2525) in 0.24.0
- Support for Python 3.11. (#2420) in 0.23.1
- Allow setting an explicit multipart boundary in `Content-Type` header. (#2278) in 0.23.1
- Allow `tuple` or `list` for multipart values, not just `list`. (#2355) in 0.23.1
- Allow `str` content for multipart upload files. (#2400) in 0.23.1
- Support connection upgrades. See https://www.encode.io/httpcore/extensions/#upgrade-requests in 0.23.1
- Don't drop empty query parameters. (#2354) in 0.23.1
- Support for [the SOCKS5 proxy protocol](https://www.python-httpx.org/advanced/proxies/#socks) via [the `socksio` package](https://github.com/sethmlarson/socksio). (#2034) in 0.22.0
- Support for custom headers in multipart/form-data requests (#1936) in 0.22.0
- Don't perform unreliable close/warning on `__del__` with unclosed clients. (#2026) in 0.22.0
- Fix `Headers.update(...)` to correctly handle repeated headers (#2038) in 0.22.0
- Fix streaming uploads using `SyncByteStream` or `AsyncByteStream`. Regression in 0.21.2. (#2016) in 0.21.3
- HTTP/2 support for tunnelled proxy cases. (#2009) in 0.21.2
- Improved the speed of large file uploads. (#1948) in 0.21.2
- The `response.url` property is now correctly annotated as `URL`, instead of `Optional[URL]`. (#1940) in 0.21.1
- The command-line client will now display connection information when `-v/--verbose` is used. in 0.21.0
- The command-line client will now display server certificate information when `-v/--verbose` is used. in 0.21.0
- The command-line client is now able to properly detect if the outgoing request should be formatted as HTTP/1.1 or HTTP/2, based on the result of the HTTP/2 negotiation. in 0.21.0
- Curio support is no longer currently included. Please get in touch if you require this, so that we can assess priorities. in 0.21.0
- The 0.20.0 release adds an integrated command-line client, and also includes some design changes. The most notable of these is that redirect responses are no longer automatically followed, unless specifically requested. in 0.20.0
- This design decision prioritises a more explicit approach to redirects, in order to avoid code that unintentionally issues multiple requests as a result of misconfigured URLs. in 0.20.0
- If you do want auto-redirect behaviour, you can enable this either by configuring the client instance with `Client(follow_redirects=True)`, or on a per-request basis, with `.get(..., follow_redirects=True)`. in 0.20.0
- This change is a classic trade-off between convenience and precision, with no "right" answer. See [discussion #1785](https://github.com/encode/httpx/discussions/1785) for more context. in 0.20.0
- The other major design change is an update to the Transport API, which is the low-level interface against which requests are sent. Previously this interface used only primitive datastructures, like so... in 0.20.0
- Now the interface is much simpler... in 0.20.0
- The `allow_redirects` flag is now `follow_redirects` and defaults to `False`. in 0.20.0
- The `raise_for_status()` method will now raise an exception for any responses except those with 2xx status codes. Previously only 4xx and 5xx status codes would result in an exception. in 0.20.0
- The low-level transport API changes to the much simpler `response = transport.handle_request(request)`. in 0.20.0
- The `client.send()` method no longer accepts a `timeout=...` argument, but the `client.build_request()` does. This required by the signature change of the Transport API. The request timeout configuration is now stored on the request instance, as `request.extensions['timeout']`. in 0.20.0
- Added the `httpx` command-line client. in 0.20.0
- Response instances now include `.is_informational`, `.is_success`, `.is_redirect`, `.is_client_error`, and `.is_server_error` properties for checking 1xx, 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx response types. Note that the behaviour of `.is_redirect` is slightly different in that it now returns True for all 3xx responses, in order to allow for a consistent set of properties onto the different HTTP status code types. The `response.has_redirect_location` location may be used to determine responses with properly formed URL redirects. in 0.20.0
- `response.iter_bytes()` no longer raises a ValueError when called on a response with no content. (Pull #1827) in 0.20.0
- The `'wsgi.error'` configuration now defaults to `sys.stderr`, and is corrected to be a `TextIO` interface, not a `BytesIO` interface. Additionally, the WSGITransport now accepts a `wsgi_error` configuration. (Pull #1828) in 0.20.0
- Follow the WSGI spec by properly closing the iterable returned by the application. (Pull #1830) in 0.20.0
- Add support for `Client(allow_redirects=<bool>)`. (Pull #1790) in 0.19.0
- Add automatic character set detection, when no `charset` is included in the response `Content-Type` header. (Pull #1791) in 0.19.0
- Event hooks are now also called for any additional redirect or auth requests/responses. (Pull #1806) in 0.19.0
- Strictly enforce that upload files must be opened in binary mode. (Pull #1736) in 0.19.0
- Strictly enforce that client instances can only be opened and closed once, and cannot be re-opened. (Pull #1800) in 0.19.0
- Drop `mode` argument from `httpx.Proxy(..., mode=...)`. (Pull #1795) in 0.19.0
- Support for Python 3.10. (Pull #1687) in 0.18.2
- Expose `httpx.USE_CLIENT_DEFAULT`, used as the default to `auth` and `timeout` parameters in request methods. (Pull #1634) in 0.18.2
- Support [HTTP/2 "prior knowledge"](https://python-hyper.org/projects/hyper-h2/en/v2.3.1/negotiating-http2.html#prior-knowledge), using `httpx.Client(http1=False, http2=True)`. (Pull #1624) in 0.18.2
- Clean up some cases where warnings were being issued. (Pull #1687) in 0.18.2
- Prefer Content-Length over Transfer-Encoding: chunked for content=<file-like> cases. (Pull #1619) in 0.18.2
- Update brotli support to use the `brotlicffi` package (Pull #1605) in 0.18.1
- Ensure that `Request(..., stream=...)` does not auto-generate any headers on the request instance. (Pull #1607) in 0.18.1
- Pass through `timeout=...` in top-level httpx.stream() function. (Pull #1613) in 0.18.1
- Map httpcore transport close exceptions to httpx exceptions. (Pull #1606) in 0.18.1
- Transport instances now inherit from `httpx.BaseTransport` or `httpx.AsyncBaseTransport`, and should implement either the `handle_request` method or `handle_async_request` method. (Pull #1522, #1550) in 0.18.0
- The `response.ext` property and `Response(ext=...)` argument are now named `extensions`. (Pull #1522) in 0.18.0
- The recommendation to not use `data=<bytes|str|bytes (a)iterator>` in favour of `content=<bytes|str|bytes (a)iterator>` has now been escalated to a deprecation warning. (Pull #1573) in 0.18.0
- Drop `Response(on_close=...)` from API, since it was a bit of leaking implementation detail. (Pull #1572) in 0.18.0
- When using a client instance, cookies should always be set on the client, rather than on a per-request basis. We prefer enforcing a stricter API here because it provides clearer expectations around cookie persistence, particularly when redirects occur. (Pull #1574) in 0.18.0
- The runtime exception `httpx.ResponseClosed` is now named `httpx.StreamClosed`. (#1584) in 0.18.0
- The `httpx.QueryParams` model now presents an immutable interface. There is a discussion on [the design and motivation here](https://github.com/encode/httpx/discussions/1599). Use `client.params = client.params.merge(...)` instead of `client.params.update(...)`. The basic query manipulation methods are `query.set(...)`, `query.add(...)`, and `query.remove()`. (#1600) in 0.18.0
- The `Request` and `Response` classes can now be serialized using pickle. (#1579) in 0.18.0
- Handle `data={\"key\": [None|int|float|bool]}` cases. (Pull #1539) in 0.18.0
- Support `httpx.URL(**kwargs)`, for example `httpx.URL(scheme=\"https\", host=\"www.example.com\", path=\"/')`, or `httpx.URL(\"https://www.example.com/\", username=\"tom@gmail.com\", password=\"123 456\")`. (#1601) in 0.18.0
- Support `url.copy_with(params=...)`. (#1601) in 0.18.0
- Add `url.params` parameter, returning an immutable `QueryParams` instance. (#1601) in 0.18.0
- Support query manipulation methods on the URL class. These are `url.copy_set_param()`, `url.copy_add_param()`, `url.copy_remove_param()`, `url.copy_merge_params()`. (#1601) in 0.18.0
- The `httpx.URL` class now performs port normalization, so `:80` ports are stripped from `http` URLs and `:443` ports are stripped from `https` URLs. (#1603) in 0.18.0
- The `URL.host` property returns unicode strings for internationalized domain names. The `URL.raw_host` property returns byte strings with IDNA escaping applied. (#1590) in 0.18.0
- Fix Content-Length for cases of `files=...` where unicode string is used as the file content. (Pull #1537) in 0.18.0
- Fix some cases of merging relative URLs against `Client(base_url=...)`. (Pull #1532) in 0.18.0
- The `request.content` attribute is now always available except for streaming content, which requires an explicit `.read()`. (Pull #1583) in 0.18.0
- Type annotation on `CertTypes` allows `keyfile` and `password` to be optional. (Pull #1503) in 0.17.1
- Fix httpcore pinned version. (Pull #1495) in 0.17.1
- Add `httpx.MockTransport()`, allowing to mock out a transport using pre-determined responses. (Pull #1401, Pull #1449) in 0.17.0
- Add `httpx.HTTPTransport()` and `httpx.AsyncHTTPTransport()` default transports. (Pull #1399) in 0.17.0
- Add mount API support, using `httpx.Client(mounts=...)`. (Pull #1362) in 0.17.0
- Add `chunk_size` parameter to `iter_raw()`, `iter_bytes()`, `iter_text()`. (Pull #1277) in 0.17.0
- Add `keepalive_expiry` parameter to `httpx.Limits()` configuration. (Pull #1398) in 0.17.0
- Add repr to `httpx.Cookies` to display available cookies. (Pull #1411) in 0.17.0
- Add support for `params=<tuple>` (previously only `params=<list>` was supported). (Pull #1426) in 0.17.0
- Add missing `raw_path` to ASGI scope. (Pull #1357) in 0.17.0
- Tweak `create_ssl_context` defaults to use `trust_env=True`. (Pull #1447) in 0.17.0
- Properly URL-escape WSGI `PATH_INFO`. (Pull #1391) in 0.17.0
- Properly set default ports in WSGI transport. (Pull #1469) in 0.17.0
- Properly encode slashes when using `base_url`. (Pull #1407) in 0.17.0
- Properly map exceptions in `request.aclose()`. (Pull #1465) in 0.17.0
- Support literal IPv6 addresses in URLs. (Pull #1349) in 0.16.1
- Force lowercase headers in ASGI scope dictionaries. (Pull #1351) in 0.16.1
- Preserve HTTP header casing. (Pull #1338, encode/httpcore#216, python-hyper/h11#104) in 0.16.0
- Drop `response.next()` and `response.anext()` methods in favour of `response.next_request` attribute. (Pull #1339) in 0.16.0
- Closed clients now raise a runtime error if attempting to send a request. (Pull #1346) in 0.16.0
- Add Python 3.9 to officially supported versions. in 0.16.0
- Type annotate `__enter__`/`__exit__`/`__aenter__`/`__aexit__` in a way that supports subclasses of `Client` and `AsyncClient`. (Pull #1336) in 0.16.0
- Add `response.next_request` (Pull #1334) in 0.15.5
- Support direct comparisons between `Headers` and dicts or lists of two-tuples. Eg. `assert response.headers == {"Content-Length": 24}` (Pull #1326) in 0.15.4
- Fix automatic `.read()` when `Response` instances are created with `content=<str>` (Pull #1324) in 0.15.4
- Fixed connection leak in async client due to improper closing of response streams. (Pull #1316) in 0.15.3
- Fixed `response.elapsed` property. (Pull #1313) in 0.15.2
- Fixed client authentication interaction with `.stream()`. (Pull #1312) in 0.15.2
- ASGITransport now properly applies URL decoding to the `path` component, as-per the ASGI spec. (Pull #1307) in 0.15.1
- Added support for curio. (Pull https://github.com/encode/httpcore/pull/168) in 0.15.0
- Added support for event hooks. (Pull #1246) in 0.15.0
- Added support for authentication flows which require either sync or async I/O. (Pull #1217) in 0.15.0
- Added support for monitoring download progress with `response.num_bytes_downloaded`. (Pull #1268) in 0.15.0
- Added `Request(content=...)` for byte content, instead of overloading `Request(data=...)` (Pull #1266) in 0.15.0
- Added support for all URL components as parameter names when using `url.copy_with(...)`. (Pull #1285) in 0.15.0
- Neater split between automatically populated headers on `Request` instances, vs default `client.headers`. (Pull #1248) in 0.15.0
- Unclosed `AsyncClient` instances will now raise warnings if garbage collected. (Pull #1197) in 0.15.0
- Support `Response(content=..., text=..., html=..., json=...)` for creating usable response instances in code. (Pull #1265, #1297) in 0.15.0
- Support instantiating requests from the low-level transport API. (Pull #1293) in 0.15.0
- Raise errors on invalid URL types. (Pull #1259) in 0.15.0
- Cleaned up expected behaviour for URL escaping. `url.path` is now URL escaped. (Pull #1285) in 0.15.0
- Cleaned up expected behaviour for bytes vs str in URL components. `url.userinfo` and `url.query` are not URL escaped, and so return bytes. (Pull #1285) in 0.15.0
- Drop `url.authority` property in favour of `url.netloc`, since "authority" was semantically incorrect. (Pull #1285) in 0.15.0
- Drop `url.full_path` property in favour of `url.raw_path`, for better consistency with other parts of the API. (Pull #1285) in 0.15.0
- No longer use the `chardet` library for auto-detecting charsets, instead defaulting to a simpler approach when no charset is specified. (#1269) in 0.15.0
- Swapped ordering of redirects and authentication flow. (Pull #1267) in 0.15.0
- `.netrc` lookups should use host, not host+port. (Pull #1298) in 0.15.0
- Removed the `URLLib3Transport` class no longer exists. We've published it instead as an example of [a custom transport class](https://gist.github.com/florimondmanca/d56764d78d748eb9f73165da388e546e). (Pull #1182) in 0.15.0
- Drop `request.timer` attribute, which was being used internally to set `response.elapsed`. (Pull #1249) in 0.15.0
- Drop `response.decoder` attribute, which was being used internally. (Pull #1276) in 0.15.0
- `Request.prepare()` is now a private method. (Pull #1284) in 0.15.0
- The `Headers.getlist()` method had previously been deprecated in favour of `Headers.get_list()`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- The `QueryParams.getlist()` method had previously been deprecated in favour of `QueryParams.get_list()`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- The `URL.is_ssl` property had previously been deprecated in favour of `URL.scheme == "https"`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- The `httpx.PoolLimits` class had previously been deprecated in favour of `httpx.Limits`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- The `max_keepalive` setting had previously been deprecated in favour of the more explicit `max_keepalive_connections`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- The verbose `httpx.Timeout(5.0, connect_timeout=60.0)` style had previously been deprecated in favour of `httpx.Timeout(5.0, connect=60.0)`. It is now fully removed. in 0.15.0
- Support for instantiating a timeout config missing some defaults, such as `httpx.Timeout(connect=60.0)`, had previously been deprecated in favour of enforcing a more explicit style, such as `httpx.Timeout(5.0, connect=60.0)`. This is now strictly enforced. in 0.15.0
- `http.Response()` may now be instantiated without a `request=...` parameter. Useful for some unit testing cases. (Pull #1238) in 0.14.3
- Add `103 Early Hints` and `425 Too Early` status codes. (Pull #1244) in 0.14.3
- `DigestAuth` now handles responses that include multiple 'WWW-Authenticate' headers. (Pull #1240) in 0.14.3
- Call into transport `__enter__`/`__exit__` or `__aenter__`/`__aexit__` when client is used in a context manager style. (Pull #1218) in 0.14.3
- Support `client.get(..., auth=None)` to bypass the default authentication on a clients. (Pull #1115) in 0.14.2
- Support `client.auth = ...` property setter. (Pull #1185) in 0.14.2
- Support `httpx.get(..., proxies=...)` on top-level request functions. (Pull #1198) in 0.14.2
- Display instances with nicer import styles. (Eg. <httpx.ReadTimeout ...>) (Pull #1155) in 0.14.2
- Support `cookies=[(key, value)]` list-of-two-tuples style usage. (Pull #1211) in 0.14.2
- Ensure that automatically included headers on a request may be modified. (Pull #1205) in 0.14.2
- Allow explicit `Content-Length` header on streaming requests. (Pull #1170) in 0.14.2
- Handle URL quoted usernames and passwords properly. (Pull #1159) in 0.14.2
- Use more consistent default for `HEAD` requests, setting `allow_redirects=True`. (Pull #1183) in 0.14.2
- If a transport error occurs while streaming the response, raise an `httpx` exception, not the underlying `httpcore` exception. (Pull #1190) in 0.14.2
- Include the underlying `httpcore` traceback, when transport exceptions occur. (Pull #1199) in 0.14.2
- The `httpx.URL(...)` class now raises `httpx.InvalidURL` on invalid URLs, rather than exposing the underlying `rfc3986` exception. If a redirect response includes an invalid 'Location' header, then a `RemoteProtocolError` exception is raised, which will be associated with the request that caused it. (Pull #1163) in 0.14.1
- Handling multiple `Set-Cookie` headers became broken in the 0.14.0 release, and is now resolved. (Pull #1156) in 0.14.1
- Our HTTP/2 support is now fully optional. **You now need to use `pip install httpx[http2]` if you want to include the HTTP/2 dependencies.** in 0.14.0
- Our HSTS support has now been removed. Rewriting URLs from `http` to `https` if the host is on the HSTS list can be beneficial in avoiding roundtrips to incorrectly formed URLs, but on balance we've decided to remove this feature, on the principle of least surprise. Most programmatic clients do not include HSTS support, and for now we're opting to remove our support for it. in 0.14.0
- Our exception hierarchy has been overhauled. Most users will want to stick with their existing `httpx.HTTPError` usage, but we've got a clearer overall structure now. See https://www.python-httpx.org/exceptions/ for more details. in 0.14.0
- When upgrading you should be aware of the following public API changes. Note that deprecated usages will currently continue to function, but will issue warnings. in 0.14.0
- You should now use `httpx.codes` consistently instead of `httpx.StatusCodes`. in 0.14.0
- Usage of `httpx.Timeout()` should now always include an explicit default. Eg. `httpx.Timeout(None, pool=5.0)`. in 0.14.0
- When using `httpx.Timeout()`, we now have more concisely named keyword arguments. Eg. `read=5.0`, instead of `read_timeout=5.0`. in 0.14.0
- Use `httpx.Limits()` instead of `httpx.PoolLimits()`, and `limits=...` instead of `pool_limits=...`. in 0.14.0
- The `httpx.Limits(max_keepalive=...)` argument is now deprecated in favour of a more explicit `httpx.Limits(max_keepalive_connections=...)`. in 0.14.0
- Keys used with `Client(proxies={...})` should now be in the style of `{"http://": ...}`, rather than `{"http": ...}`. in 0.14.0
- The multidict methods `Headers.getlist()` and `QueryParams.getlist()` are deprecated in favour of more consistent `.get_list()` variants. in 0.14.0
- The `URL.is_ssl` property is deprecated in favour of `URL.scheme == "https"`. in 0.14.0
- The `URL.join(relative_url=...)` method is now `URL.join(url=...)`. This change does not support warnings for the deprecated usage style. in 0.14.0
- One notable aspect of the 0.14.0 release is that it tightens up the public API for `httpx`, by ensuring that several internal attributes and methods have now become strictly private. in 0.14.0
- These changes should not affect users who have been working from the `httpx` documentation. in 0.14.0
- Added dedicated exception class `httpx.HTTPStatusError` for `.raise_for_status()` exceptions. (Pull #1072) in 0.14.0
- Added `httpx.create_ssl_context()` helper function. (Pull #996) in 0.14.0
- Support for proxy exclusions like `proxies={"https://www.example.com": None}`. (Pull #1099) in 0.14.0
- Support `QueryParams(None)` and `client.params = None`. (Pull #1060) in 0.14.0
- Use `httpx.codes` consistently in favour of `httpx.StatusCodes` which is placed into deprecation. (Pull #1088) in 0.14.0
- Usage of `httpx.Timeout()` should now always include an explicit default. Eg. `httpx.Timeout(None, pool=5.0)`. (Pull #1085) in 0.14.0
- Switch to more concise `httpx.Timeout()` keyword arguments. Eg. `read=5.0`, instead of `read_timeout=5.0`. (Pull #1111) in 0.14.0
- Use `httpx.Limits()` instead of `httpx.PoolLimits()`, and `limits=...` instead of `pool_limits=...`. (Pull #1113) in 0.14.0
- Keys used with `Client(proxies={...})` should now be in the style of `{"http://": ...}`, rather than `{"http": ...}`. (Pull #1127) in 0.14.0
- The multidict methods `Headers.getlist` and `QueryParams.getlist` are deprecated in favour of more consistent `.get_list()` variants. (Pull #1089) in 0.14.0
- `URL.port` becomes `Optional[int]`. Now only returns a port if one is explicitly included in the URL string. (Pull #1080) in 0.14.0
- The `URL(..., allow_relative=[bool])` parameter no longer exists. All URL instances may be relative. (Pull #1073) in 0.14.0
- Drop unnecessary `url.full_path = ...` property setter. (Pull #1069) in 0.14.0
- The `URL.join(relative_url=...)` method is now `URL.join(url=...)`. (Pull #1129) in 0.14.0
- The `URL.is_ssl` property is deprecated in favour of `URL.scheme == "https"`. (Pull #1128) in 0.14.0
- Add missing `Response.next()` method. (Pull #1055) in 0.14.0
- Ensure all exception classes are exposed as public API. (Pull #1045) in 0.14.0
- Support multiple items with an identical field name in multipart encodings. (Pull #777) in 0.14.0
- Skip HSTS preloading on single-label domains. (Pull #1074) in 0.14.0
- Fixes for `Response.iter_lines()`. (Pull #1033, #1075) in 0.14.0
- Ignore permission errors when accessing `.netrc` files. (Pull #1104) in 0.14.0
- Allow bare hostnames in `HTTP_PROXY` etc... environment variables. (Pull #1120) in 0.14.0
- Settings `app=...` or `transport=...` bypasses any environment based proxy defaults. (Pull #1122) in 0.14.0
- Fix handling of `.base_url` when a path component is included in the base URL. (Pull #1130) in 0.14.0
- Include missing keepalive expiry configuration. (Pull #1005) in 0.13.3
- Improved error message when URL redirect has a custom scheme. (Pull #1002) in 0.13.3
- Include explicit "Content-Length: 0" on POST, PUT, PATCH if no request body is used. (Pull #995) in 0.13.2
- Add `http2` option to `httpx.Client`. (Pull #982) in 0.13.2
- Tighten up API typing in places. (Pull #992, #999) in 0.13.2
- Fix pool options deprecation warning. (Pull #980) in 0.13.1
- Include `httpx.URLLib3ProxyTransport` in top-level API. (Pull #979) in 0.13.1
- Use `httpcore` for underlying HTTP transport. Drop `urllib3` requirement. (Pull #804, #967) in 0.13.0
- Rename pool limit options from `soft_limit`/`hard_limit` to `max_keepalive`/`max_connections`. (Pull #968) in 0.13.0
- The previous private "Dispatch API" has now been promoted to a public "Transport API". When customizing the transport use `transport=...`. The `ASGIDispatch` and `WSGIDispatch` class naming is deprecated in favour of `ASGITransport` and `WSGITransport`. (Pull #963) in 0.13.0
- Added `URLLib3Transport` class for optional `urllib

It looks like you encountered an error. The error message "module 'os' has no attribute 'openat'" suggests that the 'os' module in your Python environment is missing the 'openat' attribute. This could be due to a few reasons:

1.  **Python Version:** The `openat` function was introduced in Python 3.3. If you are using an older version of Python, you won't have access to it.
2.  **Operating System Compatibility:** `openat` is a POSIX-specific function. If you are on a non-POSIX system (like older versions of Windows), it might not be available.

Could you please check your Python version and operating system? This information will help us figure out the next steps.
